title: withdrawToNativeChain trusts nominal deposit amount/token and can execute underfunded withdrawals
reason: Merged into existing F-004 as a broader underfunded-input variant (ETH-sentinel bypass plus nominal-amount accounting).

title: GatewaySend destination execution ignores ERC20 transfer success values
reason: Merged into F-005; unchecked transfer results are one mechanism of the broader reserve-drain issue.

title: Fee calculation error in _swapAndSendERC20Tokens approves wrong amount leading to potential token theft
reason: As written, excess-theft path is unsupported: gateway spending uses actual swap input (`amounts[0]`) approvals; mismatch primarily causes conservative reverts, not over-withdrawal theft.

title: Missing slippage protection and minReturnAmount validation in swap operations
reason: `minReturnAmount`/`deadline` are passed into external swap routers that enforce them; user-chosen trade parameters are not a protocol-level vulnerability by themselves.

title: Unchecked externalId collision in revert/abort handlers causes refund overwrite
reason: Overbroad as written: GatewayCrossChain now blocks overwrite (`REFUND_INFO_ALREADY_EXISTS`); only GatewayTransferNative side is retained as F-011.

title: GatewayCrossChain claimRefund deletes refundInfo after transfer allowing reentrancy via callback
reason: Incorrect for current code: GatewayCrossChain `claimRefund` deletes storage before external transfer, preventing same-entry reentrancy payout.

title: Missing destination chain (dstChainId) validation allows routing to invalid chains
reason: `dstChainId` is not the primary routing primitive for token withdrawal in these paths; invalid values mainly change branch behavior and lead to reverts/refunds, not direct exploitable asset loss.

title: No deadline enforcement allows stale swaps
reason: Unsupported: deadline is forwarded to swap routers, where expiry checks are expected to occur.
